primary_drivers: PHX and MIN generated enough second-half scoring despite PHX shooting inefficiency

halftime_snapshot.entry_note: User locked Over 167.5 at -127, slightly above stated -125 cap. Managed as yellow micro-position.

MIN.N_Howard: 14 pts, 11 reb, 4 OREB, 4 TOV, 4 PF; high-rebound profile with foul/turnover volatility.
MIN.N_Coffey: 13 pts, 11 reb, 6 PF; strong efficiency but foul-out risk confirmed.
MIN.O_Miles: 13 pts, 6 reb, 7 ast; creation stabilized MIN offense.
MIN.E_Cechova: 11 bench pts, 6 reb; bench scoring helped over sustain.

PHX.K_Copper: 30 pts on 9-22 FG, 10-13 FT; primary scoring engine and foul pressure source.
PHX.D_Bonner: 16 pts, 7 reb; secondary scoring stable despite poor team FG%.
PHX.A_Thomas: 10 pts, 4 reb, 8 ast on 2-10 FG and 6-13 FT; facilitation supported pace, scoring efficiency weak.
PHX.N_Mack: 9 pts, 10 reb, 3 OREB, 3 blk; interior activity supported second-chance environment.

rebound_environment.summary: MIN dominated total boards 43-31, but both teams generated 9 offensive rebounds and 13 second-chance points. Rebound environment supported total sustain through extra possessions.
notes: MIN defensive rebounding advantage prevented PHX from running away despite turnover pressure.
Equal OREB totals and equal second-chance points created enough supplemental scoring for over path.
PHX poor FG% did not kill total because FT volume and transition points replaced field-goal efficiency.

modification_needed.PRICE_CAP_EXCEPTION_LOGIC: Entry was -127 despite user max -125 rule. Future workflow should hard-block -126 or worse unless user explicitly overrides in-message before placement.
